source: 英语_作文_未知_中考英语作文预测（华一一度老师制作）.pdf
question:
你的英国笔友对中国的智能生活很感兴趣，请你写一篇短文介绍 AI 如何改变你的生活。

内容包括：
1. AI 在你生活中的具体应用（居家、学习、出行等）
2. 这些应用给你带来的便利
3. 你的感受

注意：
1. 词数：90 左右
2. 短文需包括所给要点，可适当发挥
3. 短文中不得出现真实姓名和校名

提示词汇：
AI (Artificial Intelligence) 人工智能
smart speaker 智能音箱，convenient 方便的

范文：AI and Smart Life
AI is changing my life in many ways. It makes my daily life more convenient and interesting.
At home, I use a smart speaker to play music and set alarms. When I study, AI apps help me check my homework and find learning resources. When I go out, I can pay by phone quickly and use map apps to find the way easily.
I think AI is a great helper. It not only saves me a lot of time but also makes my life more colorful. I hope AI will bring us more surprises in the future.

source: 英语_作文_未知_中考英语作文预测（华一一度老师制作）.pdf
question:
现在，绿色低碳、保护环境已成为全社会的共识。请你写一篇短文，谈谈我们中学生应该如何践行绿色低碳生活，为环保贡献力量。

内容要点：1.我们践行绿色低碳生活的原因
2.中学生可以做的具体环保行动
3.你的呼吁与期望

要求：1.词数90左右
2.短文必须包括所有要点，可适当发挥
3.文中不得出现真实姓名、校名

提示词汇：green and low-carbon 绿色低碳
protect the environment 保护环境
save energy 节约能源, recycle 回收利用

参考范文：
Live a Green and Low-Carbon Life

Protecting the environment is our duty. Living a green and low-carbon life can help make our earth better and cleaner.

As students, we can do many small things. We can go to school on foot or by bike instead of taking cars. We should save water and electricity in daily life. We can also recycle paper and plastic bottles to reduce waste.

I hope everyone can join us to live a green life. Let's work together to protect our home and make it more beautiful.
figure_description: 页面右侧有一幅插图：三名中学生骑自行车上学，前方一名男生（短发、白色校服上衣、深色短裤、背书包）骑自行车微笑前行，身后两名同学（男生着浅色上衣、女生着白色上衣）也骑自行车跟随，背景为蓝天白云和绿树。

question:
预测六、劳动教育与实践

劳动教育是成长的重要一课，能让我们在实践中学会动手、懂得珍惜。作为中学生，我们应积极参与劳动实践，在劳动中收获成长。请你写一篇短文分享你的劳动经历与感悟。

内容要点：
1. 你参与过的劳动实践（家庭、学校、社区）
2. 劳动实践带给你的收获
3. 你对劳动教育与实践的理解

要求：
1. 词数90左右
2. 短文必须包括所有要点，可适当发挥
3. 文中不得出现真实姓名、校名

提示词汇：labor education 劳动教育, practice 实践, work 劳动, help 帮助, learn 学习, value 珍惜

范文：Labor Education and Practice
Labor education is very important for our growth. It teaches us to work hard and value the fruits of labor. I take part in labor practice actively in daily life.
At home, I help my parents clean the house and wash dishes. At school, I join in the labor class and plant trees with my classmates. These practices make me independent and responsible.
Through labor practice, I know labor is great. It helps us grow better. We should keep working hard and learn more from labor practice.

【逐段译文】：
劳动教育与实践
劳动教育对我们的成长非常重要，它教会我们努力劳动，珍惜劳动成果。我在日常生活中积极参与劳动实践。
在家里，我帮父母打扫房子、洗碗。在学校，我参加劳动课，和同学们一起种树。这些实践让我变得独立、有责任心。
通过劳动实践，我知道劳动是伟大的，它帮助我们更好地成长。我们应该坚持努力劳动，从劳动实践中学到更多。
answer:
Labor Education and Practice
Labor education is very important for our growth. It teaches us to work hard and value the fruits of labor. I take part in labor practice actively in daily life.
At home, I help my parents clean the house and wash dishes. At school, I join in the labor class and plant trees with my classmates. These practices make me independent and responsible.
Through labor practice, I know labor is great. It helps us grow better. We should keep working hard and learn more from labor practice.

source: 英语_作文_未知_中考英语作文预测（华一一度老师制作）.pdf
question:
预测八、未来规划与梦想
每个人都有⾃⼰的梦想，合理的未来规划能帮助我们实现梦想。作为中学生，树⽴明确的梦想、做好规划，能让我们在成长路上更有⽅向。请你写⼀篇短⽂，分享你的梦想和未来规划。
内容要点：1.你的梦想是什么（贴合中学生，简单易写）
2.你为实现梦想制定的具体规划
3.你对实现梦想的决⼼和期待
要求：1.词数90左右 2.短⽂必须包括所有要点，可适当发挥
3.⽂中不得出现真实姓名、校名
提示词汇：dream 梦想, future 未来, plan 规划,
study hard 努⼒学习, try one's best 尽最⼤努⼒,
come true 实现
⽂章标题：My Dream and Future Plan
answer:
范文：My Dream and Future Plan
Everyone has a dream, and I have mine too. My dream is to be a teacher because I want to help more students learn and grow.
To make my dream come true, I have a clear plan. I will study hard every day and learn all subjects well. I will also practice speaking and communicating to become a good teacher. I will never give up when I meet difficulties.
I believe with my hard work, my dream will come true one day. I look forward to my future and will keep moving towards my dream.
【逐段译文】：
我的梦想与未来规划
每个人都有梦想，我也⼀样。我的梦想是成为⼀名⽼师，因为我想帮助更多的学⽣学习和成⻓。
为了实现我的梦想，我有⼀个明确的规划。我会每天努⼒学习，学好所有科⽬。我也会练习说话和沟通，成为⼀名优秀的⽼师。遇到困难时，我永远不会放弃。
我相信，通过我的努⼒，我的梦想总有⼀天会实现。我期待我的未来，并会朝着我的梦想不断前进。

question:
【题目】现在乡村发展越来越好，家乡变得更加美丽宜居。请你写一篇短文，介绍家乡的变化，表达对家乡的热爱。
内容要点：1.家乡在环境、生活等方面的变化  2.你能为家乡做的事情  3.希望家乡的未来更加美好
要求：1.词数90左右  2.短文必须包括所有要点，可适当发挥  3.文中不得出现真实姓名、校名
提示词汇：hometown 家乡，change 变化，clean 干净的，happy 幸福的，beautiful 美丽的

【范文】My Beautiful Hometown
My hometown is becoming more and more beautiful these years. Great changes have taken place. The roads are wide and clean. The houses look new and tidy. People live a happy and healthy life. The environment is much better than before.
I love my hometown very much. I will study hard to learn more knowledge. I want to do something useful for my hometown.
I hope my hometown will be even nicer in the future. I am sure it will have a bright future.

【逐段译文】：
我美丽的家乡
这些年我的家乡变得越来越美了。它发生了巨大的变化。道路宽阔干净，房子崭新整洁。人们过着幸福健康的生活，环境比以前好很多。
我非常热爱我的家乡。我会努力学习知识，想为家乡做些有用的事。
我希望家乡未来会更加美好，我相信它一定会有光明的未来。

source: 英语_作文_未知_中考英语作文预测（华一一度老师制作）.pdf
question:
网络已成为我们生活的一部分，网络文明与安全至关重要。作为中学生，我们要学会文明上网、保护自己，共同营造安全、健康的网络环境。请你写一篇短文分享你的做法与看法。
内容要点：
1.网络给我们带来的便利
2.我们应如何做到文明上网、保障网络安全
3.呼吁大家共同守护网络环境
要求：1.词数90左右　2.短文必须包括所有要点，可适当发挥　3.文中不得出现真实姓名、校名
提示词汇：network 网络, civilization 文明, safety 安全, use 使用, protect 保护, healthy 健康的

【范文】：Network Civilization and Safety
The network is very useful in our daily life. It helps us study and get information easily, but we must pay attention to network civilization and safety.
When using the network, we should be polite and not say bad words. We must not share our personal information with strangers. We should also stay away from bad information online.
It's our duty to keep the network environment healthy. Let's work together to use the network in a civilized and safe way.

【逐段译文】：
网络文明与安全
网络在我们的日常生活中非常有用，它帮助我们轻松学习、获取信息，但我们必须重视网络文明与安全。
使用网络时，我们要讲礼貌，不说脏话。我们不能向陌生人透露个人信息，还要远离网上的不良信息。
守护健康的网络环境是我们的责任，让我们一起努力，文明、安全地使用网络。

question:
In Chongqing, a mother broke the TV because her kid didn't listen to her and kept watching it while eating. The news shows how many kids today can't stop using phones, tablets, or watching TV too much. The mother's action shows her anger, but it also teaches the child a bad lesson. Parents should guide kids wisely instead of using force (暴力) because real education (教育) comes from understanding, not from being afraid.

An 80-Year-Old's Fight for Care

An 80-year-old Chinese woman took her two sons and a daughter to court (法庭) because they didn't take care of her. Though she raised them with great love, her children now pay no attention to her. In China, looking after parents is both a law and a good tradition. But today, some people care more about themselves than their family. This thing shows how family traditions are becoming less important.

In 2024, Wang, a full-time housewife, was injured (受伤) in a traffic accident caused by Xu. Although Wang didn't have a job outside, she was taking care of her family. She asked for lost-work pay, but Xu said no, saying she wasn't really working. However, the court in Wuhu thought differently. It believed that Wang's housework saved money for the family. So, the court said yes to her lost-work pay.

1. In which SECTION of the newspaper can we read the news?
A. LIFE.	B. SPORTS.	C. TRAVEL.	D. HEALTH.

Animals love playing hide-and-seek! To stay safe, they hide by looking like the things around them. In forests, a mantis（螳螂）___1___ among leaves. Under the ocean, a goby fish matches the sandy seafloor ___2___. Rainforests have hiding animals, too. Look closely—that ___3___ "is a leaf katydid（螽斯）!" question:
阅读下面短文，在空白处填入一个适当的词，或填入括号中所给单词的正确形式。

Have you ever dreamed of flying over traffic jams? In China, this dream is coming true. A new industry called the "low-altitude economy (低空经济)" is growing fast in China. It includes flying taxis ___1___ are powered by electricity and can take off straight up. Over 70% of the ___2___ (world) patents (专利) in this field are owned by China. This industry was thought to be worth 1.5 trillion (1 trillion = 1000 billion) yuan by 2025.

The Chinese government strongly supports this new field. More than 30 provinces ___3___ (make) simpler rules for drones and flying machines already. It shows the government really takes this industry ___4___ (serious).

Drones are already used ___5___ many ways now. For example, unmanned passenger flights are tested by ___6___ (company) like EHang, which brings flying taxis closer to reality. Things ___7___ (send) by drones in busy cities. That saves a lot of time.

___8___ there are not many courses about low-altitude technology now, it's certain that there will be more in the future. More and more schools and universities plan ___9___ (offer) some new courses and also teach students how to operate drones.

In a word, a new way of travel is created in China. The low-altitude economy, which once seemed like ___10___ science fiction movie, is now becoming a part of our real life step by step.
figure_description: 文章开头配有一架四旋翼无人机的卡通插图，无人机下方吊着一个包裹（标注drone），用于辅助说明低空经济主题。Passage 1共10个填空：1.定语从句关系代词；2.用括号内world的正确形式；3.用括号内make的正确形式；4.用括号内serious的正确形式；5.填入适当介词；6.用括号内company的正确形式；7.用括号内send的正确形式；8.填入适当连词；9.用括号内offer的正确形式；10.填入适当冠词。

The 34-year-old influencer（网红）made a surprising comeback by posting two new videos online. They became ___2___ popular that her return quickly became a hot topic on the website. Her different kinds of ___3___ (ability) attracted millions of people. And thanks to her, now Chinese lacquerware techniques（漆器工艺）are ___4___ (know) by people all over the world again.
Li started posting short videos on Sina Weibo in 2016. They were simply about rural life with her grandmother in Sichuan Province. In the videos, Li often dresses in graceful ___5___ (tradition) clothes, rises at sunrise, rests at sunset, plants seeds and ___6___ (flower), cooks Chinese dishes and crafts bamboo furniture. No other bloggers（博主）could show ___7___ more peaceful life than her. During her absence（隐退）, many of her fans wondered where she was and ___8___ or not she had retired from posting. In the latest interview, Li said she mainly relaxed and spent time with her grandmother over the past three years. She also visited more than 100 intangible cultural heritage inheritors（非遗传人）in 20 provinces to get inspiration（灵感）for new videos in the future. ___9___ an unofficial cultural ambassador（大使）, Li plays an important role in ___10___ (spread) Chinese art.

question:
六、综合填空 (共10小题，每小题1分，满分10分)
阅读下面短文，根据所给首字母、上下文或括号内单词等提示，在空白处填入适当的单词。每个空只能填一个词。
As we know, Yunnan is a kingdom of wild fungi (真菌) that can be eaten. But do you know that fungi play an important role in environment? And they are a source (来源) of food and medicine. But w____________(76) do we know little about them?
About two million kinds of fungi-more than 90% of all-have yet to be ____________(77) (describe) by science, according to Rebecca Morelle, a science reporter for the BBC. In the UK alone, there are thought to be around 25, 000 kinds of fungi -five or six ____________(78) (time) more than plants. Scientists are trying to find out more about this largely unknown world, but it is challenging. Dr Martyn Ainsworth, a senior researcher from Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, in London, says, "We can grow a c____________(79) number in the lab, but there are a lot of fungi we cannot grow in the lab. This has ____________(80) (hold) back our research. "
The fact that we know so little about fungi l____________(81) many of us to misunderstand them. A lot of people are a ____________(82) of fungi. But only a very small percent of fungi in the world can c____________(83) disease. Most are harmless, called by some "the hidden helpers of our environment". Some fungi provide food and shelter (庇护处) ____________(84) wildlife, help plants take in water and nutrients (营养) and recycle (回收利用) waste and dead matter. O____________(85) fungi grow on the roots of trees and plants, forming a huge underground network that helps trees to grow. Fungi are really the behind-the-scenes team that are doing all the work.
So, next time you add mushrooms to your dinner, take a painkiller for a headache, or go for a walk through the woods, remember to thank the living things that make it all possible- fungi.

阅读下面三篇材料，从每题所给的A、B、C、D四个选项中，选出最佳选项。

A
Tearless Onions Go on Sale
UK supermarket Waitrose has started selling a new type of "tearless" onions called Sunions. Many people find that chopping onions makes them cry. This is because onions give off chemicals that make your eyes water. Plant breeder Rick Waston crossed several types of onions to create a version that was the least pungent (刺激性的). There's just one catch---they are three times more expensive than Waitrose's cheapest own-brand onions.

"See" with a Bionic (仿生) Eye
An 88-year-old woman who was blind in her left eye can now see with it again---thanks to a microchip placed under her retina. The retina is a light-sensitive (感光的) area at the back of the eye. The chip picks up video signals from a camera fitted on a special pair of glasses. The signals are then sent to the brain, which understands them as if they were produced by natural vision. The patient, who has received the implant (移植),

A
Qingshan Library
Wuhan Qingshan Library's new building opened to the public on May 22. The three-floor library covers an area of 8,600 square meters and has seats for over 400 library users. To better provide services, the library also offers a "wish list" service to readers: they can write the titles of their favorite books on a wish list, and the library will buy the books from the local Xinhua Bookstore. The library is open between 9 a.m. and 6 p.m. (closed on Mondays).
New Wuhan Library Project
The project of the new Wuhan Library started. The library will be at the Wangjiadun CBD (Central Business District). With an investment of RMB 1.8 billion, the library's floor area will be 100,000 square meters, three times the size of the current(现有的) library on Jianshe Avenue. The project will be finished in 2024.
Tang Lake Library in Wuhan is the most beautiful library in the city. It is the first library built in a park. The

Many people love smelling an old book. But what makes that special smell? Lignin (木质素)! Lignin can be 41___ in the trees that paper is made from. As a book gets older, lignin starts to break down slowly and 42___ chemicals we can smell. But why do people like the smell? Scientists from University College London carried out a study where they 43___ an "old book smell" using fibers (纤维) from a 1928 French book and seventy-nine people were asked to smell it. question:
Meng Xiang was crazy about films and music during his childhood. When he started college, he dreamed to make films that could combine(结合) his love for storytelling and interest in music.

His biggest challenge came when he planned to make a film about a tennis player. Since no one would offer him money for a 2-hour film, Meng and his college friends tried to make a shorter one. They bought a second-hand camera and used old things instead of expensive equipment(设备). They even made a shelf on their own to support the camera.

Meng and his friends worked hard, rain or shine. When filming the tennis player, they ran after him in the summer sun. If it rained and they couldn't film outside, they hung bed sheets(床单) on the walls in an empty room to set up a simple studio(摄影棚). This 12-minute film got much attention. The teachers and students in their college liked the music they created for it.

After that, Meng had another idea: to make a musical film. But musicals weren't popular in college, and many said it wouldn't work. Instead of listening to the doubts, Meng and his team spent weeks writing new songs and making a test video. To film the moving scenes, they fixed a smartphone on a skateboard and pushed it. The actors' hair was blowing in the wind while they were dancing and singing. The loud voice and laughter of the team filled the air.

Their musical film was well received and won several awards at a film festival. As Meng said, "Making films isn't about having the best equipment or famous actors. It's about finding new ways to tell your story. Our team is planning to make a special documentary about environment. And I can't wait to write music for it."

question:
Passage C:
Do you know the five main senses we have? They are touch, taste, sight, smell and hearing. They are controlled by different parts of our brains. So we call the brain our built-in "BeiDou". Brains help our bodies work smoothly. Try this: close your eyes and touch your nose. It's easy, right? That's because our brains can receive messages about where our body parts are. We can also stand on one leg without falling down. If we lose our balance, our brains will give us orders to fix it.

★. Tests show that people see spider pictures faster than flowers. Some researchers also found that people react (作出反应) to loud noises faster than to a friend's voice. Scientists aren't completely sure about the reasons. Some suggest that this skill might come from ancient times. Our ancestors were often faced with predators. So they had to protect themselves from dangerous animals like spiders and tigers, or they would lose their lives. Until today, we still jump at sudden noises or shadows (影子) in dark rooms.

Here is more about brains. Can you imagine seeing colors when you hear music, or tasting candy when you see the sunshine? It's called sense mixing. Scientists think this happens when different senses intermingle (相互交织). Another fact is that temperature sometimes influences our senses. Some people can feel storms coming through knee pain. So we often joke that we're "human weather apps". Also, our brains are able to make adjustment (调整) to some senses. If someone can't see well, his hearing or touch might become stronger.

Though scientists have done much research on the brain, there's still a lot to do. Have you ever wondered why some people are better at sports? What's the secret to improving memory? Then it's time for you to start your discovering journey.

[situations / thought / active / refused / born / special/ because of]

Two baby giraffes have been born this year — both without a single spot(斑点)! The first was born at Brights Zoo in Tennessee, USA. It was ___66___ to be the only giraffe in the world without a spot. But another spotless baby giraffe was ___67___ in southern Africa. It is very ___68___ because it's the first ever spotless giraffe recorded in the wild. Wow! So what's causing these strange ___69___? Well, experts think the strange coloring might be ___70___ a small change in the calves'(小鹿) DNA that makes their whole coat brown.
figure_description: 图片显示两只长颈鹿在动物园的围栏内，是两只没有斑点的幼年长颈鹿的黑白照片。

The reason ___73___ tanghulu is so popular is because it tastes really good and it can be made with many different ingredients(食材). In China, people say that "anything can be made into tanghulu". The Internet ___74___(help) make tanghulu famous in South Korea. Many teenagers and young ___75___(adult) have seen tanghulu on the Internet and want to try it. The popularity of tanghulu in South Korea gives ___77___(we) some inspiration. No matter how delicious the food is, it has to be adapted(适应) to the local customs, especially ___78___ going abroad. The eating habits ___79___ consumers(消费者)in other countries should be thought about. Tanghulu has actually experienced a "localization" process in South Korea.

Integrating(使融合) ___80___(tradition) Chinese food with South Korean eating habits, tanghulu brings not only a mixture of taste but also culture.

question:
Sally hoped to explore the outside world because _______.
A. she was not satisfied with her life
B. she wanted to be a director
C. she was interested in it
D. she had a scholarship
figure_description: 阅读材料B内容：Sally grew up in a small country in Southeast Asia. She wanted very much to explore（探索）the big world one day. When she was in her late twenties, she got a scholarship（奖学金）to study films in England. However, life in England was different from the films and television shows. Everything was expensive. Not understanding the culture, she made many mistakes. With only a little English, she felt separated from others. Finally, Sally moved to London and met different people from other parts of the world. Like her, they were still learning English and dealing with culture shocks. Those made her decide to try writing real growing-up stories in English, which moved many people who had similar situations like her. Sally was not lonely anymore. When Sally turned 30, she believed, "It is when you start achieving your goals: the films you wanted to make, the books you wanted to write, the places you still wanted to see." Sally wrote more books in English and won several awards. More people enjoyed her books, and she was invited to talk at writers' festivals and teach in universities. Her writing and her films had connected her to the world! Though she was in England, her heart was deeply rooted（根植）in her hometown. After Sally gave birth to her son at 40 in 2013. She decided to visit her hometown more often and spared no effort on film education there.

source: 英语_试卷_2024_2024_武汉教科院2024届元调九年级适应性训英语试题_2.pdf
question:
When Oxford University Press (出版社) chose "rizz" as its 2023 Word of the Year, many people asked, "Rizz? Is there such a word?" At the same time, many young English speakers laughed out loud "Jeepers, haven't you ever heard of rizz?"

Rizz means the ability to attract a person. Are you good at making friends? Do your classmates often laugh loudly at your jokes? Then you probably have rizz. As in "to rizz up someone", it means to attract someone. The word first appeared on social media as teen slang(俚语) and became popular after the Spider-Man actor Tom Holland said he had "no rizz."

___★___ "Jeepers" is another. People use it to express shock. And the word "tea" is no longer just a "drink". It also means "gossip(闲聊)." Similarly, here in China, words like "xianyanbao"have a new meaning. Teens are also saying "dazi"( pal or friend) and "qimengle"(What?).

These changes show that both English and Chinese are alive. A healthy language, researchers say, should always see new words being created, words being borrowed from other languages and old words taking on new meanings. The changes also show that young people have more influences on society.

When we look back on 2023, one thing stands out: artificial intelligence(AI). Computers have become cleverer than ever. Some AI systems(系统) have "rizzed up" many users with their ability to write long articles, create poems and draw pictures. But they can only use language. Can ChatGPT coin a slang word and make it popular? Can it give a word new meanings? Can it change how we humans speak and write?

You should be proud of your ability to play with words. But while you try to create another slang word, remember to be polite. Also, don't use slang in your compositions - it won't "rizz up" your teachers!

(本题为阅读C篇正文，文中★处为某题设空标记，本页未显示该小题具体题干)
figure_description: 阅读C篇正文配有一张黑白图，位于右上方，图中文字为"Word Of The Year 2023"，采用立体艺术字体风格，"2023"中'0'字设计为类似齿轮或表情符号的圆形图案，整体呈装饰性标题样式。

Once scientists used underwater cameras to film them and they ___42___ them throwing things at each other. The octopuses often throw shells (贝壳) away by their ___43___ after eating. Sometimes, they even ___44___ plants or sand and let them go while letting out a jet (喷射) of water. They might be doing this to keep their space ___45___, but often they hit other octopuses nearby. After studying the videos, scientists believe they are doing this on purpose.
answer: 41. together; 42. caught; 43. arms; 44. pick up; 45. clean

56. 假如你是李华，你做志愿活动的国际社区邀请你写一封英文倡议书，倡导大家"祥和过春节"。请你从春节的意义、庆祝方式、安全注意事项等内容方面完成倡议书。
内容提示：
What does Spring Festival mean to Chinese?
How do people usually celebrate Spring Festival?
Can you give some suggestions about a joyful and safe festival?
Something more about it.
注意：文中不得透露个人真实信息；词数100词左右；开头已给出，不计入总词数。
_________________________________________
_________________________________________
_________________________________________
_________________________________________
_________________________________________
参考答案：
A Letter for a Joyful and Safe Spring Festival
Dear friends,
  Spring Festival is a time for family reunion. It's also a time to be grateful for what we have and to look forward to the new year with hope. To celebrate it, there are a few things we can do.

question:
本学期，你们班即将开展"My Own Robot（我的专属机器人）"设计活动，大家可以自主设计不同用途的机器人，如家务助手机器人（housework helper robot）、学习助手机器人（study helper robot）、陪伴机器人（companion robot）等。请你根据以下提示写一篇短文，参加学校英语报的专题征文活动。
内容提示：
1. Which kind of robot do you want to design?
2. Why do you want to design it?
3. What special things can it do for people?
4. Please write more about it.
注意：文中不得透露个人真实信息；词数100词左右；标题和开头已给出，不计入总词数。
My_____Robot
I_____want to design a/an_____
answer:
【答案】例文
My Study Helper Robot
I want to design a study helper robot, for I really need such a helpful partner in my daily life. For example, when I have trouble with maths problems, it will explain the steps patiently until I really understand. It can also make a daily study plan based on my weak points, so I won't waste time on what I've already mastered. What I like most is that it can remind me to rest when I've studied too long. In this way, it helps me keep a balance between study and health. With this robot by my side, learning will no longer be a lonely or tiring task.

question:
【详解】总体分析
①题材：本文是一篇材料作文；
②时态：主要时态为"一般现在时"；
③提示：写作要点已给出，考生应注意不要遗漏"信息提示"中的每一项提示，适当添加细节，并突出写作重点。
[写作步骤]
第一步，引出"如何健康地生活"的话题；
第二步，从饮食、睡眠、体育锻炼及如何面对生活中的苦恼等方面具体阐述写作内容；
第三步，书写结语，表达期待。
[亮点词汇]
①be good for 对……有好处
②at least 至少
③additionally 此外
[高分句型]
①Moreover, it is necessary to do sports for at least an hour every day.（it作形式主语）
②I hope everyone can have a healthy body and mind and enjoy a better life.（宾语从句）

question:
五、词与短语填空（共5小题，每小题2分，满分10分）
仔细阅读下面短文，然后用下面方框中所给的单词或短语填空，使每个句子在结构、句义和逻辑上正确。（提示：选项中有两个是多余的）
You may know about "junk food" like French fries, but what about "junk sleep"? A British survey shows that electronic products in teenagers' bedrooms are ____66____ their sleep.
The survey was done among 1,000 British kids from 12 to 16. It found that 50% of them got just 4 to 7 hours' sleep every day. They often fell ____67____ while using electronic products like a phone, an iPad or a computer and then became tired the next day.
"This is very ____68____," said Dr. Chris Idzikowski, a British professor. "Junk sleep brings two problems—____69____ to have enough sleep and have low sleep quality(质量). So, children should ____70____ electronic products when going to bed."

source: 英语_资料_2026_2026_英语（湖北武汉）（考试版）.pdf
question:
六、阅读理解填词（共10小题，每小题1分，满分10分）
阅读下面材料，在空白处填入适当的内容（1个单词）或括号内单词的正确形式。
When I arrived in China, the first Chinese word I learned was about food. I learned the names of a few ____71____(dish), but still didn't know most of the things I saw on Chinese restaurant menus. Luckily, there were pictures ____72____(help) me.
Nearly every restaurant menu in China has pictures of food in it. ____73____ many restaurants I've been to in the US don't. What is the reason for the ____74____(different)?
One thing I've definitely (明确地) noticed about China is that food is a ____75____(big) part of their culture than it is in the US. A common way to greet someone in China is to ask them ____76____ they've eaten yet.
Food is just a bigger deal here and ____77____(have) pictures of food in menus is part of that. Some menus are almost like works of art, with well-framed photos.
Another factor is the names of the dishes. Chinese dishes sometimes have fanciful (别出心裁的) names like "palace guard chicken" or "three fresh things from the soil". These are ____78____(interest) names, but they don't really tell you what the dishes are like. Therefore, it's helpful to have pictures so you can actually see what you're going to be eating.
There's ____79____(probable) even more to it than that. All I know is that I was very grateful to have those picture menus ____80____ I first came here.

question:
Sleep like a giraffe. Giraffenap is made to improve performance at work through short sleep. A Japanese company says that it will allow the user to be energetic in just 20 minutes. Inside the box are four points to help the body stand. That means he can rest his arms, legs and head in the box while still remaining standing, just like a giraffe.

Word bank: asleep / influencing / worrying / requiring / deal with / keep away from / fail

source: 英语_资料_2026_2026_英语（湖北武汉）（考试版）.pdf
question:
"Seeing" the world in animals' eyes. Do you want to know what the world is like in the eyes of animals? A team of scientists has made a special camera. It uses the technology of photography (成像) to "see" lights at different lengths. This includes lights that we can't see with our eyes, while animals can see them. This way, the camera can record videos as if through the eyes of animals.

Word bank: asleep / influencing / worrying / requiring / deal with / keep away from / fail
